steps.title: Identify the key signal linking the two sentences
steps.content:
The first sentence calls a quad axel an “extreme feat.” The second describes the audience's response: “the audience was left awestruck.” The strongest clue is “awestruck”—an extreme emotional reaction that matches the extreme difficulty already introduced.

The transition must show why this reaction is appropriate for such a feat.

steps.title: Select the transition that expresses the appropriate relationship
steps.content:
”Fittingly” (B)—meaning “appropriately”—captures that an awestruck response is the appropriate reaction to such extreme difficulty.

The other three don't express this connection: “moreover” adds new points, “next” marks sequence, and “however” falsely implies contrast.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
